Hey, welcome back and welcome to the module on Advanced Body Language, the second of those dual linchpins of unconscious communication. Let me start by saying that of all the tactics and strategies that make up the straight line system, body language is definitely the most amusing one. We human beings were particularly quirky bunching, this very quirkiness expresses itself at the absolute highest level when it comes to the rules and guidelines that govern unconscious communication in the form of body language. However that being said, don't think for a second that this quirkiness and how amusing it is diminishes from the importance of mastering the art of effective body language. I mean frankly if you don't master this stuff or you've become proficient at it, it's going to end up tripping you up again and again in both sales and in life in general as you try to go about getting people into rapport with you and also establishing yourself as an expert in your field during those all important first four seconds. In fact the best way to explain this is to have you take a quick trip down memory lane, go all the way back to grade school, maybe the third or fourth grade, remember one or two of those kids in your class that were just really off in some way. To the point where the thought of being friends with them or really even staying next to them would almost send the shiver down your spine or at least gross you out as the phrase goes, right? Well as adults of course we know better now, we also hopefully are more empathetic. We know we're not going to become like someone simply because they look weird or if we stand too close to them, right? My point though is that if you think back to what it actually was that grossed you out so much back then, did it really have anything to do with the actual words they said? No, it was not that. It was based on how they looked, the way they dressed, how they moved their body, how they made eye contact. You see on the flip side now, let's talk about an experience I had later in life. I was actually in Mongolia of all places giving a seminar and you know people in Mongolia are Mongolians, right? You know they're nice people but very much average the way they dress, the way they look, the way they carry themselves and I was in a room about a thousand of them. They all wanted to shake my hand, take pictures and out of the corner of my eye I noticed one man who's really well dressed, perfectly fitting suit, quaffed hair, looks good, eyes just strong. I'm like that guy's the most important guy in the room. He's the lead and sure enough I was dead on balls accurate. He was the country's most famous athlete or wrestler and everybody was just like gravitating to this one guy but by the way I didn't know he was famous or was this big time wrestler. It was the second eye laid eyes. This guy here was his suit, it was his tie, it was his watch. Not that they were that expensive, it was how they fit, how he stood, how his eyes were just so piercing. That's body language and it's actually very similar to tonality in the sense that if you have flawless body language on every level it won't directly result in you actually closing a sale. Where body language will stop you from closing a sale if it's awful body language to hold death by a thousand cut syndrome with each error in your body language being a wound and a wound and a wound and another wound until before you know what your people are so wound in basically so turned off or repulsed by you they're just like sorry pal it's not interesting I can't do it. That's the result of negative body language. In essence nonverbal communication is actually 10 times more powerful than verbal communication and it hits you with the force of a cannonball right in the gut. Things like thoughts, feelings, intentions they're all communicated by how you move your body, by your management of space and time, by your posture, your appearance, the gestures you make, your various facial expressions, your level of eye contact and even the way you smell. Now again if you recall from module number two an unconscious communication body language hits you in literally one twenty fourth of a second and it actually tested this biomechanically by putting people into those brain imaging machines those PET scanners and they know that when you see something or someone it instantly goes a close to light speed to the occipital lobe back here where you actually see and then bam a quarter of a second later it goes to the prefrontal cortex where an actual judgment gets made. That's how fast this happens and it's almost exclusively based on those patterns that will lay down in our unconscious minds during our childhood. Insofar as what looks right, what looks wrong, what makes sense, what doesn't make sense, what does an expert look like, what does a novice look like, how does an expert move their body, how do they dress, how do they make eye contact, how do they shake hands. How are they perceived? Remember when the prospect lays eyes on you for the first time now one twenty fourth of a second they begin the process of ripping you apart, judging each piece of you and they put you back together based on how you were perceived. If you perceive the right way well then you set yourself up for a massive downhill slide easy time fit. If you perceive the wrong way good luck trying to close the sale. That's why body language is so crucial and that's what this module is all about. So with that let's head to lesson number one. No quiz for you right here. Let's rock and roll and meet me in the next lesson and we'll head forward. See you soon.
